name: Artigo
classification_notes: Artigo draws directly from the humanist roman tradition, taking cues from the handwriting qualities of early renaissance typefaces. Its italics lean noticeably calligraphic, placing it firmly in the old-style category.
summary: A contemporary old-style text serif by Joana Correia, originating from the MA Typeface Design programme at the University of Reading, with strong calligraphic italics and coverage across Latin, Cyrillic and Greek.
description: Rooted in the handwriting qualities of early roman types, Artigo is built for small text where personality matters. Six weights from Light to Extrabold each carry matching italics that grow progressively more expressive, with the display version leaning furthest toward the handwritten. The Greek extension won second prize at the Granshan Awards in 2011, and the family now covers an extensive range of Latin languages alongside Cyrillic.
foundry_blurb: Nova Type describe Artigo as an old-style inspired typeface system for text, conceived at the University of Reading in 2011 and developed to support Greek and Cyrillic. They note its strength in small text with personality, and highlight the italics as capturing the family's dynamic, handwritten quality most fully.

name: Anona
classification_notes: Anona is an upright italic sans serif with visible handwriting influences, giving it a humanist warmth that distinguishes it from geometric or grotesque models. The organic letterforms and calligraphic construction place it firmly in the humanist sans category.
summary: An upright italic sans serif by Joana Correia with handwriting-influenced letterforms aimed at packaging and playful brand identities.
description: Designed by Joana Correia and released in 2023, Anona draws on calligraphic construction to produce a sans serif whose upright roman styles carry the character of italic forms. The family spans nine weights from Thin to Black, each with a matching italic, and covers Latin Extended, Cyrillic and Greek. Stylistic sets expose alternate letterforms that shift the tone between fruity and warm, making it useful for identity and packaging work that wants personality without overt script fussiness.
foundry_blurb: Nova Type Foundry describe Anona as an upright italic sans serif that brings handwriting shapes into a sans, making it well suited to packaging and playful identities. They encourage exploring the stylistic sets for different character shapes, and note a complementary slanted version alongside the upright cuts.

name: Alga
classification_notes: Alga is described as a high-contrast modern typeface, with contrast increasing significantly from the lighter to bolder weights, placing it squarely in the modern or didone serif tradition.
summary: A high-contrast modern serif by Joana Correia, designed around tall proportions and expressive curves suited to headlines and display work.
description: Designed by Joana Correia and released in 2020, Alga began as an exploration of a tall, elegant form in the lighter weights before evolving into a high-contrast serif at bolder settings. The family spans six weights from ExtraLight to Bold, each with a matching italic, giving twelve styles in total. It performs best at larger sizes and in short editorial copy, where its fancy curves and lively character read clearly.
foundry_blurb: Nova Type Foundry describe Alga as a high-contrast modern typeface with a contemporary look, featuring subtle details that make it appealing at big sizes and headlines. They note it is a lively and charming serif with fancy curves that works well in headlines and short pieces of text, and explain it originated from an exploration of a tall, elegant serif with low contrast in the thinner weight before evolving into a high-contrast design in bolder cuts, while always retaining its core style and personality.

name: Baga
classification_notes: The foundry describes Baga as a script typeface with smooth, inviting letterforms designed for display use in packaging and branding, with connected or flowing shapes characteristic of casual script styles.
summary: A soft display script by Joana Correia in five weights, aimed at packaging and branding work with smooth, rounded letterforms.
description: Designed by Joana Correia and released in 2025, Baga offers ten styles across five weights from Light to Bold, each with a matching italic. The typeface includes a stylistic set offering two-storey a and g alternates to aid legibility at smaller sizes, and covers Latin Extended for broad European language support. Its smooth character shapes suit consumer packaging, editorial display and identity work where a gentle, approachable tone is needed.
foundry_blurb: Nova Type Foundry describe Baga as a soft display typeface with five weights, whose smooth and inviting shapes make it well suited to packaging and branding. The family includes a stylistic set with two-storey a and g forms to improve legibility at smaller sizes, and covers the Latin Extended character set to extend its practical range.

name: Artigo Display
classification_notes: Described by the foundry as a contemporary interpretation of handwriting shapes in a display version of the italic, with expressive personality distinct from its text companion; classified as display given its orientation toward large-scale use and calligraphic italic structure.
summary: A display italic by Joana Correia that draws on handwriting shapes, released in six weights and awarded a Certificate of Typographic Excellence by the Type Directors Club in 2018.
description: Conceived as an expressive counterpart to the Artigo text typeface, this display family pushes the italic toward a more calligraphic register suited to headlines and large-scale setting. Six weights from ExtraLight to Black give it range across editorial and branding contexts. Oldstyle figures and ordinals are included as OpenType features, and Latin coverage extends to a broad range of European languages.
foundry_blurb: Nova Type Foundry describe Artigo Display as the odd sister of the Artigo text typeface: a contemporary interpretation of handwriting shapes rendered as a display italic with its own distinct personality. Originally released in 2017, it was later expanded with five new weights to allow more flexibility across different media. The family received a Certificate of Typographic Excellence from the Type Directors Typeface Competition in January 2018.

name: Laca Pro
classification_notes: Described by the foundry as a semi-sans serif, Laca Pro retains some serif-like qualities while operating primarily within the sans-serif genre, giving it a hybrid warmth suited to branding and editorial use.
summary: A semi-sans serif by Joana Correia, drawn from retro Portuguese soap packaging and designed to carry warmth and personality across branding and text settings.
description: Inspired by mid-century Portuguese packaging, Laca Pro sits between a conventional sans and something softer, with an upright italic available as a stylistic set for added informality. The family spans eight weights from ExtraLight to Black, each with a matching italic, and covers Latin Extended, Cyrillic and Greek. A broad set of OpenType features includes oldstyle figures, discretionary ligatures, contextual alternates and five stylistic sets.
foundry_blurb: Nova Type Foundry describe Laca Pro as a semi-sans serif inspired by retro Portuguese soap packaging, with "Laca" being the Portuguese word for hairspray. Designed by Joana Correia in 2020, the brief was to create a typeface with a familiar closeness and warmth while ensuring it works on modern platforms. The family includes an upright italic via a stylistic set, and is described as lively and versatile, suited to branding and communications.

name: Lemongrass Script
classification_notes: A connected brush script with low contrast and smooth joins, designed explicitly for display use and inspired by brush lettering traditions.
summary: A connected brush script by Joana Correia, drawn from the wind-swept energy of Porto and designed for branding and packaging.
description: Inspired by brush lettering and the coastal winds of Porto known as 'nortada', it keeps contrast low and connections smooth to maintain even rhythm across a word. The family includes a complementary Caps style designed to read more clearly in all-caps settings, extending the typeface's flexibility. OpenType features cover contextual alternates, stylistic alternates, swashes and ligatures, giving designers room to distinguish a brand's voice.
foundry_blurb: Nova Type describe Lemongrass as a connected script for display use, inspired by brush lettering and the strong northern winds of Porto. They highlight its low contrast and smooth connections as key to its rhythm, and position it for branding and packaging work. Lemongrass Caps was developed as a companion style for clearer all-caps text, and the family supports most western European languages.

name: Loretta
classification_notes: Loretta is a contemporary serif with calligraphic roots, low contrast and open counters, placing it in the transitional serif tradition suited to continuous text and editorial work.
summary: A contemporary text serif by Joana Correia and Abel Martins, with calligraphic origins restrained into stable, legible forms across six weights.
description: Designed by Abel Martins and Joana Correia and released in 2021, Loretta offers six weights from Light to ExtraBold each with a matching italic, totalling 12 styles. Low contrast and open shapes make it well-suited to body text in print and on screen, while the italic retains enough calligraphic energy to hold its own in editorial settings. OpenType features include oldstyle figures, tabular and lining figures, contextual alternates and standard ligatures.
foundry_blurb: Nova Type Foundry describe Loretta as an elegant contemporary serif for body text, rooted in expressive calligraphic motions that have been drawn into stable typographic shapes. They highlight its low contrast and open forms as qualities that give it strong legibility in books and on the web, with a vibrant italic designed to complement the regular styles.

name: Laca Text
classification_notes: Laca Text is a sans serif drawn from the base shapes of Laca, with open counters, a larger x-height and true italics, placing it firmly in the humanist sans tradition optimised for text setting.
summary: A text-oriented sans serif by Joana Correia, derived from Laca but stripped back for legibility at smaller sizes across 14 styles.
description: Derived from the Laca family, this version trades Laca's more expressive details for a larger x-height, open counters and shorter ascenders that hold up well in continuous text. The seven weights run from ExtraLight to SemiBold, each with a true italic that sits quieter than its display counterpart. Suitable for long-form editorial and identity work, and designed to pair with the original Laca or stand independently.
foundry_blurb: Nova Type Foundry describe Laca Text as a cleaner, sans serif take on Laca, retaining its round shapes while adding a bigger x-height, open counters and smaller ascenders for better performance at text sizes. They note it has true italics that are calmer than those of Laca, and position it as suited to both long-form text and identity and branding work, versatile enough to pair with Laca or be used on its own.

name: Loretta Display
classification_notes: Described as elegant and sharp, optimised for larger sizes, placing it firmly in the display serif category. Its refinement at headline scale and extended Latin coverage suggest a contemporary editorial serif with high contrast.
summary: A display-optimised serif designed by Joana Correia, offering six weights with italics for sharp, elegant headline work.
description: Loretta Display is a display-specific variant of Loretta, redrawn for larger sizes where its sharp, elegant character can read clearly. The family spans six weights from Extra Light to Bold, each with a matching italic, giving twelve styles in total. Latin Extended and partial Greek coverage make it serviceable across a range of European languages.
foundry_blurb: Nova Type describe Loretta Display as a version of Loretta designed to perform at bigger sizes, characterising it as elegant and sharp. Designed by Joana Correia with Abel Martins, they position it as an addition to an editorial typeface collection, available for desktop, webfont and app licensing.

name: Ragu
classification_notes: Ragu is classified as display due to its soft rounded construction, generous curves and distinctive terminal details aimed at branding, packaging and signage rather than extended text use.
summary: A rounded display typeface by Joana Correia and Marcus Sterz, built for branding and packaging where warmth and shelf presence matter equally.
description: Six upright weights from Extralight to Bold, each paired with a true italic, give designers a tonal range that moves from gentle to assertive while retaining consistent softness. Open counters, carefully controlled spacing and sturdy silhouettes allow Ragu to hold legibility at smaller sizes, covering ingredients lists and functional label copy as well as headlines. Distinctive terminal and curve details add craft character without disrupting the coherence needed for brand systems. Covers Latin Extended and Vietnamese.
foundry_blurb: Nova Type Foundry describe Ragu as a warm, flavorful display typeface designed for branding and packaging, where a friendly voice must remain crisp and readable in real-world conditions. Soft rounded construction and generous curves bring approachable handmade warmth, while confident weight and sturdy silhouettes give it shelf presence for labels and signage. The family spans 12 styles with OpenType features including oldstyle figures, fractions and two stylistic sets.
